DAMAGE ALONG THIS 5.6 MILE PATH WAS RELATIVELY CONTINUOUS AND OF VARYING INTENSITY. NUMEROUS HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES SUFFERED DAMAGE, EITHER BEING UPROOTED OR SNAPPED AT THE TRUNK COMPLETELY. SOME OUTBUILDINGS LOST METAL ROOF PANELS, AND ONE LESS-STURDY BUILDING LOST WALLS, CAUSING IT TO COLLAPSE COMPLETELY. THIS EF1 TORNADO TRACK ENDED ALONG CARMEL ACHOR ROAD JUST WEST OF STATE ROUTE 170.

A SECOND, DISCRETE BUT COMPACT TRACK OF DAMAGE WAS NOTED BEGINNING TO THE WEST OF THE PRIOR TORNADO TRACK ALONG CARMEL ACHOR ROAD AND COINCIDENT WITH A SEPARATE RADAR-INDICATED CIRCULATION. THIS 4.1 MILE TRACK APPEARS TO BE LIMITED TO TREE DAMAGE. THE TRACK CROSSED ROUTE 170 AND CURVED ALONG PANCAKE CLARKSON ROAD, CROSSING AT THE STATE LINE WITH PENNSYLVANIA. THE EF0 TORNADO PATH CONTINUED INTO BEAVER COUNTY PA AND RAPIDLY WEAKENED.

CONSIDERABLE TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG A SWATH FROM NEAR ROGERS THROUGH AND NORTHEAST OF EAST PALESTINE. DAMAGE WAS ESPECIALLY CONCENTRATED ALONG AND NORTH OF HOWELL ROAD, WHERE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED AND A SMALL AIR HANGAR WAS CONSIDERABLY DISFIGURED. WIND LIKELY APPROACHED 80 MPH AT TIMES ALONG THIS TRACK. DETAILS WILL BE INCLUDED IN A PNS.

A BRIEF TORNADO DEVELOPED SOUTHEAST OF HANOVERTON AND MOVED NORTHEAST ALMOST TO ROUTE 30 BEFORE DISSIPATING. THE TORNADO CAUSED MAINLY TREE DAMAGE, BUT ALSO DAMAGED A FARMSTEAD ALONG LINDESMITH ROAD, WHERE SHEET METAL ROOFING WAS REMOVED FROM A BARN, AND 3000 LB HAY WAGONS WERE DISPLACED. THIS BRIEF TORNADO WAS RATED EF0 WITH A MAXIMUM WIND SPEED OF 70-75 MPH.

COLUMBIANA COUNTY EMA REPORTED THAT A TREE WAS BLOWN ONTO A CAR WITH OCCUPANTS ALONG ROUTE 154 EAST OF ROGERS. THIS MARKS THE BEGINNING OF A SWATH OF STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE THAT CONTINUED INTO SOUTHWESTERN LAWRENCE COUNTY PA. THE DETAILS OF THIS SWATH WILL BE SUMMARIZED IN A PNS.
EF2 TORNADO OBSERVED AND CONTINUED FOR 10 MILES.
MULTIPLE TREES/POWER LINES/TRANSFORMERS BLOWN ACROSS THE COUNTY.

A SWATH OF CONSIDERABLE WIND DAMAGE WAS ENCOUNTERED FROM NEAR ROGERS IN COLUMBIANA COUNTY TO FAR WESTERN LAWRENCE COUNTY. THIS SWATH WAS CONSIDERED TO BE LIKELY NON-TORNADIC AFTER A DETAILED ANALYSIS OF RADAR AND DAMAGE. WIND SPEED AT TIMES LIKELY APPROACHED 80 MPH IN THIS AREA OWING TO ACCELERATION ON THE SOUTHEASTERN FLANK OF A MESOVORTEX. DETAILS WILL BE INCLUDED IN A PNS.
